content_markdown: A sample of water of mass $0.25\,kg$ is heated from $20^\circ \mathrm{C}$ to $28^\circ \mathrm{C}$. The specific heat capacity of water is $4.2 \times 10^3\,J\,kg^{-1}\,K^{-1}$. What is the thermal energy gained by the water?
vibe_explanation: Use $Q = mc\Delta T$. The mass, heat capacity, and temperature rise combine directly to give the transferred thermal energy.
